text: The construction of molecular complexity through the simultaneous formation of two contiguous C(sp3) stereocentres via 1,2-difunctionalization of internal alkenes is a commonly used synthetic strategy. While racemic methods have been well established, the development of asymmetric variants, particularly those using open-shell, carbon-centered radical intermediates, remains highly elusive. Herein, we disclose that enantioenriched arylsulfinylamides can be used as all-in-one reagents for the asymmetric, intermolecular aminoarylation of alkenes under very mild photoredox conditions. Using nitrogen addition to the alkene as the initial step, with a 1,4-aryl translocation to produce the desired aminoarylation adducts in high enantioselectivity, these traceless chiral auxiliaries ensure that the resulting β,β-diarylethylamines, aryl-α,β-ethylenediamines, and α-aryl-β-aminoalcohols that are common to many biologically significant molecules can be obtained in an efficient fashion with complete regio-, diastereo-, and enantioselectivity.
explanation: This reads like a polished scientific abstract with domain-specific terminology; while plausible as human-written, the even, high-level presentation is also characteristic of AI-generated summaries.
